data.content:
### Create OAuth Client for Managed Organization

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Creates an OAuth client for a specific managed organization using its API key.

```APIDOC
## POST /v2/oauth-clients

### Description
Creates an OAuth client for a managed organization. This allows the managed organization to authenticate and authorize applications.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`https://api.cal.com/v2/oauth-clients`

### Parameters
#### Headers
- **Authorization** (string) - Required - Bearer token using the managed organization's API key (e.g., `Bearer cal_xxx168ceb89117cc3a1b1f1777511506`).

#### Request Body
- **name** (string) - Required - The name of the OAuth client.
- **redirectUris** (array of strings) - Required - An array of URIs that users are redirected to after authorization.
- **permissions** (array of strings) - Required - An array of permissions to grant to the OAuth client. Use `["*"]` for all permissions.

### Request Example
```json
{
    "name": "OAuth client for managed organization",
    "redirectUris": ["https://your-domain.com*"],
    "permissions": ["*"]
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - The status of the operation ('success').
- **data** (object) - Contains the details of the created OAuth client.
  - **clientId** (string) - The unique client ID for the OAuth client.
  - **clientSecret** (string) - The secret key for the OAuth client.

#### Response Example
```json
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "clientId": "xxxepq3bg0000khghtq4bxxx",
        "clientSecret": "xxxhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IlpXVCJ9.eyJuYW1lIjoiT0F1dGggY2xpZW50IGZvciBtYW5hZ2VkIG9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbiIsInJlZGlyZWN0VXJpcyI6wyJodHRwOi8vbG9jYWxob3N0OjQzMjEiXSwicGVybWlzc2lvbnMiOjY0LCJpYXQiOjE3NDAxMzg4MjB9.3nzplyvqf-ZlMUuhX9mYfpxtneH62iJPB6WpsYBWxxx"
    }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch Specific OAuth Client

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves details of a specific OAuth client by its ID.

```APIDOC
## GET /v2/oauth-clients/:oAuthClientId

### Description
Fetches the details of a specific OAuth client using its client ID.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
`https://api.cal.com/v2/oauth-clients/:oAuthClientId`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **oAuthClientId** (string) - Required - The ID of the OAuth client to retrieve.

#### Headers
- **Authorization** (string) - Required - Bearer token using the managed organization's API key (e.g., `Bearer cal_xxx168ceb89117cc3a1b1f1777511506`).

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- An object containing the details of the OAuth client, including 'clientId', 'name', 'redirectUris', and 'permissions'.

#### Response Example
```json
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "clientId": "xxxepq3bg0000khghtq4bxxx",
        "name": "OAuth client for managed organization",
        "redirectUris": ["https://your-domain.com*"],
        "permissions": ["*"]
    }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch All OAuth Clients

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves a list of all OAuth clients associated with the authenticated managed organization.

```APIDOC
## GET /v2/oauth-clients

### Description
Fetches a list of all OAuth clients created for the authenticated managed organization.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
`https://api.cal.com/v2/oauth-clients`

### Parameters
#### Headers
- **Authorization** (string) - Required - Bearer token using the managed organization's API key (e.g., `Bearer cal_xxx168ceb89117cc3a1b1f1777511506`).

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- An array of OAuth client objects, each containing 'clientId', 'name', 'redirectUris', and 'permissions'.

#### Response Example
```json
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "clientId": "xxxepq3bg0000khghtq4bxxx",
            "name": "OAuth client for managed organization",
            "redirectUris": ["https://your-domain.com*"],
            "permissions": ["*"]
        }
    ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### OAuth Client Credentials Header Example

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/introduction.mdx

Shows how to authenticate API requests using OAuth client credentials, which include a client ID and a secret key passed in custom headers. This is specific to Cal.com's API.

```http
x-cal-client-id: YOUR_CLIENT_ID
x-cal-secret-key: YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET
```

--------------------------------

### Create OAuth Client for Managed Organization via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Creates a new OAuth client for a managed organization using a POST request to the Cal.com API. Requires the managed organization's API key for authentication. Accepts client name, redirect URIs, and permissions.

```json
{
    "name": "OAuth client for managed organization",
    "redirectUris": ["https://your-domain.com*"] ,
    "permissions": ["*"]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch All OAuth Clients for Managed Organization via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves a list of all OAuth clients associated with a managed organization via a GET request to the Cal.com API. Authentication is performed using the managed organization's API key.

```http
GET https://api.cal.com/v2/oauth-clients
Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch Specific OAuth Client for Managed Organization via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves details of a specific OAuth client for a managed organization using its ID via a GET request to the Cal.com API. Authentication requires the managed organization's API key.

```http
GET https://api.cal.com/v2/oauth-clients/:oAuthClientId
Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>
```

--------------------------------

### Cal.com Google Calendar Callback URIs

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/self-hosting/apps/install-apps/google.mdx

These are the authorized redirect URIs required for the Google OAuth flow within Cal.com. They specify where Google should send the user back after authentication. Ensure `<Cal.com URL>` is replaced with your Cal.com instance's domain.

```text
<Cal.com URL>/api/integrations/googlecalendar/callback
<Cal.com URL>/api/auth/callback/google
```

--------------------------------

### Force Refresh Tokens API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/introduction.mdx

If managed user's access or refresh tokens are lost, they can be force-refreshed using OAuth client credentials. The new tokens should then be stored.

```APIDOC
## POST /platform/managed-users/force-refresh-tokens - Force Refresh Tokens

### Description
This endpoint allows for the forceful refreshing of access and refresh tokens for a managed user, utilizing OAuth client credentials. This is useful in scenarios where existing tokens may have been lost or compromised.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/platform/managed-users/force-refresh-tokens`

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **userId** (string) - Required - The ID of the managed user whose tokens need to be refreshed.

### Request Example
```
POST /platform/managed-users/force-refresh-tokens?userId=managed_user_id
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **accessToken** (string) - The newly generated access token.
- **refreshToken** (string) - The newly generated refresh token.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "accessToken": "newly_generated_access_token",
  "refreshToken": "newly_generated_refresh_token"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create Managed User

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/quickstart.mdx

This endpoint allows you to create new managed users within your Cal.com organization using OAuth client credentials.

```APIDOC
## POST /v2/oauth-clients/:clientId/users

### Description
Creates a new managed user associated with a specific OAuth client. This allows for programmatic user creation and management within your Cal.com organization.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/v2/oauth-clients/:clientId/users`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **clientId** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of your OAuth client.

#### Headers
- **x-cal-secret-key** (string) - Required - The client secret associated with your OAuth client.

#### Request Body
- **email** (string) - Required - The email address of the user to be created.
- **name** (string) - Optional - The full name of the user.
- **timeFormat** (string) - Optional - The time format to use. Accepts `12` (for 12-hour AM/PM) or `24` (for 24-hour).
- **weekStart** (string) - Optional - The day the week starts on. Accepts `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Wednesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday`, `Saturday`, `Sunday`. Defaults to `Sunday`.
- **timeZone** (string) - Optional - The IANA timezone identifier for the user (e.g., `America/New_York`). Defaults to `Europe/London`.

### Request Example
```json
{
    "email": "bob@example.com",
    "timeZone": "America/New_York"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - Indicates the success status of the operation.
- **data** (object) - Contains the user information and authentication tokens.
  - **user** (object) - Information about the created user.
    - **id** (integer) - The unique identifier of the created user.
    - **email** (string) - The email address of the user.
    - **username** (string) - The username of the user.
  - **accessToken** (string) - The access token for the user.
  - **refreshToken** (string) - The refresh token for the user.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "user": {
      "id": 179,
      "email": "bob@example.com",
      "username": "bob"
    },
    "accessToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ0eXBlIjoiYWNjZXNzX3Rva2VuIiwiY2xpZW50SWQiOiJjbHUxZmNtYjEwMDAxa2hyN3g3ZHJleWw0Iiwib3duZXJJZCI6MTc5LCJpYXQiOjE3MTE0NDI3OTR9.EsC3JRPHQnigcp_HSijKCIp8EgcWs2kj4AFxYXYc9sM",
    "refreshToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ0eXBlIjoicmVmcmVzaF90b2tlbiIsImNsaWVudElkIjoiY2x1MWZjbWIxMDAwMWtocjd4N2RyZXlsNCIsIm93bmVySWQiOjE3OSwiaWF0IjoxNzExNDQyNzk0fQ.GjklEucgey8yWMoGz7ABntbxYdiqqQFPooQjqGd3B5I"
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Cal.com v2 API Authentication Methods

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/faq.mdx

The Cal.com v2 API supports two primary authentication methods. You can use a Bearer token for user-specific access or client credentials for OAuth client authentication. Ensure you use the correct header format for each method.

```bash
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" https://api.cal.com/v2/...
```

```bash
curl -H "x-cal-client-id: <client-id>" -H "x-cal-secret-key: <client-secret>" https://api.cal.com/v2/...
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch Managed Organization by ID via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves a specific managed organization using its ID via a GET request to the Cal.com API. Authentication is done using the manager organization's OAuth client ID and secret.

```http
GET https://api.cal.com/v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations/:managedOrganizationId
Authentication: x-cal-client-id: <OAuth client id>
x-cal-secret-key: <OAuth client secret>
```

--------------------------------

### Cal.com Environment Variable for Google Credentials

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/self-hosting/apps/install-apps/google.mdx

This environment variable stores the downloaded JSON content of your Google OAuth Client ID. It is essential for Cal.com to authenticate with Google services. Ensure the entire JSON string is pasted correctly.

```dotenv
GOOGLE_API_CREDENTIALS=<JSON_CONTENT_HERE>
```

--------------------------------

### POST /v2/api-keys/refresh

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Refreshes the API key for a managed organization. This is necessary if the API key was created with an expiration date.

```APIDOC
## POST /v2/api-keys/refresh

### Description
Refreshes the API key for a managed organization. This operation is required if the `apiKeyNeverExpires` option was not set to `true` during organization creation, and the key has reached its expiration period (defaulting to 30 days if `apiKeyDaysValid` was not specified).

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`https://api.cal.com/v2/api-keys/refresh`

### Parameters
#### Headers
- **Authorization** (string) - Required - `Bearer <apiKey>` where `<apiKey>` is the current API key of the managed organization.

#### Request Body
- **apiKeyDaysValid** (integer) - Optional - The number of days for which the new API key will be valid. If not provided, the default is 30 days.
- **apiKeyNeverExpires** (boolean) - Optional - Set to `true` to create an API key that never expires. This cannot be used in conjunction with `apiKeyDaysValid`.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "apiKeyDaysValid": 30
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - Indicates the status of the operation, expected to be "success".
- **data** (object) - Contains the refreshed API key details.
  - **apiKey** (string) - The newly generated API key for the managed organization.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "apiKey": "cal_xxx467709fffebe8c6559211cb5f178f"
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch All Managed Organizations via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves a list of all managed organizations associated with a manager organization via a GET request to the Cal.com API. Authentication requires the manager organization's OAuth client ID and secret.

```http
GET https://api.cal.com/v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations
Authentication: x-cal-client-id: <OAuth client id>
x-cal-secret-key: <OAuth client secret>
```

--------------------------------

### Refresh Managed Organization API Key (POST)

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Use this endpoint to refresh the API key for a managed organization. You must include your current API key in the Authorization header. The request body allows you to specify the validity period for the new API key or set it to never expire. The response contains the newly generated API key.

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.cal.com/v2/api-keys/refresh \
-H "Authorization: Bearer <your_current_api_key>" \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{"apiKeyDaysValid": 30}'
```

```json
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "apiKey": "cal_xxx467709fffebe8c6559211cb5f178f"
    }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Update Managed Organization via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Updates an existing managed organization using a PATCH request to the Cal.com API. Allows modification of the organization's name or metadata. Authentication is handled with the manager organization's OAuth client credentials.

```http
PATCH https://api.cal.com/v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations/:managedOrganizationId
Authentication: x-cal-client-id: <OAuth client id>
x-cal-secret-key: <OAuth client secret>

{
    "name": "updated organization name",
    "metadata": {
        "new_key": "new_value"
    }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch Managed Organizations

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves a list of all managed organizations associated with a parent organization.

```APIDOC
## GET /v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations

### Description
Fetches a list of all managed organizations belonging to the specified parent organization.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
`https://api.cal.com/v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **organizationId** (string) - Required - The ID of the parent organization.

#### Headers
- **x-cal-client-id** (string) - Required - The client ID of the parent organization's OAuth client.
- **x-cal-secret-key** (string) - Required - The secret key of the parent organization's OAuth client.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- An array of managed organization objects, each containing 'id', 'name', and 'metadata'.

#### Response Example
```json
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "id": 148,
            "name": "some organization",
            "metadata": {
                "some": "metadata"
            }
        }
    ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create Managed Organization

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Creates a new managed organization under a parent organization and returns its API key.

```APIDOC
## POST /v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations

### Description
Creates a managed organization and returns its API key. Requires prior creation of an OAuth client in the Cal.com platform.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`https://api.cal.com/v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **organizationId** (string) - Required - The ID of the parent organization.

#### Headers
- **x-cal-client-id** (string) - Required - The client ID of the pre-created OAuth client.
- **x-cal-secret-key** (string) - Required - The secret key of the pre-created OAuth client.

#### Request Body
- **name** (string) - Required - The name of the managed organization.
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Custom metadata for the organization.
- **apiKeyDaysValid** (integer) - Optional - The number of days the generated API key will be valid. Defaults to 30.
- **apiKeyNeverExpires** (boolean) - Optional - If true, the generated API key will never expire.

### Request Example
```json
{
    "name": "some organization",
    "metadata": {
        "some": "metadata"
    },
    "apiKeyDaysValid": 60
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - The status of the operation ('success').
- **data** (object) - Contains the details of the created managed organization.
  - **id** (integer) - The unique identifier of the managed organization.
  - **name** (string) - The name of the managed organization.
  - **metadata** (object) - The metadata associated with the organization.
  - **apiKey** (string) - The API key for the managed organization. This key is crucial for subsequent operations.

#### Response Example
```json
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "id": 148,
        "name": "some organization",
        "metadata": {
            "some": "metadata"
        },
        "apiKey": "cal_xxx168ceb89117cc3a1b1f1777511506"
    }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create Managed Organization via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Creates a new managed organization using a POST request to the Cal.com API. Requires manager organization's OAuth client ID and secret for authentication. Accepts organization name, optional metadata, and API key validity period.

```json
{
    "name": "some organization",
    "metadata": {
        "some": "metadata"
    },
    "apiKeyDaysValid": 60
}
```

--------------------------------

### Update Managed Organization

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Updates the name or metadata of a specific managed organization.

```APIDOC
## PATCH /v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations/:managedOrganizationId

### Description
Updates the name or metadata of an existing managed organization.

### Method
PATCH

### Endpoint
`https://api.cal.com/v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations/:managedOrganizationId`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **organizationId** (string) - Required - The ID of the parent organization.
- **managedOrganizationId** (string) - Required - The ID of the managed organization to update.

#### Headers
- **x-cal-client-id** (string) - Required - The client ID of the parent organization's OAuth client.
- **x-cal-secret-key** (string) - Required - The secret key of the parent organization's OAuth client.

#### Request Body
- **name** (string) - Optional - The new name for the managed organization.
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - The new metadata for the managed organization.

### Request Example
```json
{
    "name": "updated organization name",
    "metadata": {
        "new_key": "new_value"
    }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- An object containing the updated details of the managed organization.

#### Response Example
```json
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "id": 148,
        "name": "updated organization name",
        "metadata": {
            "new_key": "new_value"
        }
    }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch Managed Organization by ID

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves details of a specific managed organization by its ID.

```APIDOC
## GET /v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations/:managedOrganizationId

### Description
Fetches the details of a specific managed organization using its unique ID.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
`https://api.cal.com/v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations/:managedOrganizationId`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **organizationId** (string) - Required - The ID of the parent organization.
- **managedOrganizationId** (string) - Required - The ID of the managed organization to retrieve.

#### Headers
- **x-cal-client-id** (string) - Required - The client ID of the parent organization's OAuth client.
- **x-cal-secret-key** (string) - Required - The secret key of the parent organization's OAuth client.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- An object containing the details of the managed organization, including 'id', 'name', and 'metadata'.

#### Response Example
```json
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "id": 148,
        "name": "some organization",
        "metadata": {
            "some": "metadata"
        }
    }
}
```
```
